title: CaseBrief
description: FIRAC+ case-brief output (spec §5.2). Every claim cites a source Block id.

case_name.description: e.g., 'Shelley v. Kraemer'. Must match the case_opinion_block.block_metadata.case_name exactly when present.

citation.description: Canonical reporter citation, e.g., '334 U.S. 1'. Null if unavailable.

facts.description: Ordered list of key facts, each tied to a source block.

procedural_posture.description: Who sued whom + what court is reviewing. Empty string + limitation entry if silent.

issue.description: Phrased as a question. Do NOT use 'clearly' — Pollack pet peeve (Appendix A).

rule.description: Prefer verbatim quote; mark paraphrase with '(paraphrase)'.

reasoning.description: Ordered reasoning steps from rule to holding.

where_this_fits.description: Doctrinal arc context from TOC + notes. Null/empty when unavailable.

likely_emphasis.description: Only populated when a professor_profile was provided. Ties profile pet peeves/framings to this case.

limitations.description: Claims the student might be tempted to make that the source does not support. Honesty marker.

sources.description: Deduplicated set of Block ids cited in any field above. Empty when the brief was generated from the model's general knowledge (no casebook text available).

from_general_knowledge.description: True when the casebook text was unavailable (empty / mis-extracted opinion block) and the brief was generated from the LLM's general knowledge of US case law. UI surfaces a clear badge when this is true.

source_block_ids.description: Block ids citing the casebook. May be empty when the brief is generated from the model's general knowledge (casebook text unavailable) — in that case `from_general_knowledge` is true at the top level and the UI flags the brief accordingly.

paraphrase.description: True when the text is a paraphrase of source material rather than verbatim.
